message.content:
You are working in /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster. You must write code. NEVER use `cd`.

## Task: Simplify depot_create inline guide and improve error UX

Edit `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbw/rbgp_Payor.sh`.

### 1. Replace the verbose inline guide in depot_create with a terse prompt

The current depot_create function has a verbose inline guide at lines 587-612. Replace the block from line 577 (the rubric repo URL validation step) through line 613 (after the token read) with a simplified version.

The current code (lines 577-613):
```bash
  buc_step 'Validate Rubric Repo URL (GitLab required)'
  local -r z_rubric_repo_url="${RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL:-}"
  test -n "${z_rubric_repo_url}" || buc_die "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL not set in rbrr.env"

  # Validate GitLab URL — CB v2 gitlabConfig requires gitlab.com
  case "${z_rubric_repo_url}" in
    https://gitlab.com/*) ;;
    *) buc_die "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not a gitlab.com URL — only GitLab is supported for CB v2 connections (GitHub classic PATs grant repo scope across ALL repositories; GitLab project access tokens are inherently repository-scoped)" ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Read GitLab project access token from stdin'
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "This command needs one value via stdin: a GitLab project access token."
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Rubric repo: ${z_rubric_repo_url}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Why GitLab (not GitHub)?"
  buc_info "  GitHub classic PATs grant 'repo' scope across ALL repositories the token"
  buc_info "  owner can access. Fine-grained PATs are not supported by CB v2 connections"
  buc_info "  (Google Issue Tracker #343223837). GitLab project access tokens are inherently"
  buc_info "  repository-scoped — they cannot access resources outside the associated project."
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Setup (if not already done):"
  buc_info "  1. Create a GitLab account at gitlab.com (if needed)"
  buc_info "  2. Create a project for the rubric repo"
  buc_info "  3. Create a project access token:"
  buc_bare "     Project Settings → Access Tokens → Add new token"
  buc_info "     - Name: descriptive (e.g. 'rb-depot')"
  buc_info "     - Role: Maintainer"
  buc_info "     - Scopes: api (includes read_api)"
  buc_info "     - Click 'Create project access token' and copy immediately"
  buc_info ""
  local z_gitlab_token=""
  buc_info "Paste GitLab project access token:"
  read -r z_gitlab_token || buc_die "Failed to read token from stdin"
  test -n "${z_gitlab_token}" || buc_die "Token is empty"
  buc_log_args "GitLab project access token read from stdin"
```

Replace with this simplified version:
```bash
  buc_step 'Validate Rubric Repo URL (GitLab required)'
  local -r z_rubric_repo_url="${RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL:-}"
  if test -z "${z_rubric_repo_url}"; then
    buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not set in rbrr.env"
    buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo first:"
    buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
    buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
  fi

  # Validate GitLab URL — CB v2 gitlabConfig requires gitlab.com
  case "${z_rubric_repo_url}" in
    https://gitlab.com/*) ;;
    *)
      buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not a gitlab.com URL"
      buc_info "Only GitLab is supported for CB v2 connections."
      buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo:"
      buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
      buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Read GitLab project access token from stdin'
  buc_info "Rubric repo: ${z_rubric_repo_url}"
  local z_gitlab_token=""
  buc_info "Paste GitLab project access token (see guide: ./tt/rbw-Pgl.*):"
  read -r z_gitlab_token || buc_die "Failed to read token from stdin"
  if test -z "${z_gitlab_token}"; then
    buc_info "Need a token? Run the setup guide:"
    buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
    buc_die "Token is empty"
  fi
  buc_log_args "GitLab project access token read from stdin"
```

Use the Edit tool to make this replacement. The old_string should be the exact text from the file that needs replacing. Read the file first to get the exact content.

input.new_string:
buc_step 'Validate Rubric Repo URL (GitLab required)'
  local -r z_rubric_repo_url="${RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL:-}"
  if test -z "${z_rubric_repo_url}"; then
    buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not set in rbrr.env"
    buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo first:"
    buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
    buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
  fi

  # Validate GitLab URL — CB v2 gitlabConfig requires gitlab.com
  case "${z_rubric_repo_url}" in
    https://gitlab.com/*) ;;
    *)
      buc_warn "RBRR_RUBRIC_REPO_URL is not a gitlab.com URL"
      buc_info "Only GitLab is supported for CB v2 connections."
      buc_info "Set up a GitLab rubric repo:"
      buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
      buc_die "Cannot proceed without GitLab rubric repo"
      ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Read GitLab project access token from stdin'
  buc_info "Rubric repo: ${z_rubric_repo_url}"
  local z_gitlab_token=""
  buc_info "Paste GitLab project access token (see guide: ./tt/rbw-Pgl.*):"
  read -r z_gitlab_token || buc_die "Failed to read token from stdin"
  if test -z "${z_gitlab_token}"; then
    buc_info "Need a token? Run the setup guide:"
    buc_next "rbw-Pgl"
    buc_die "Token is empty"
  fi
  buc_log_args "GitLab project access token read from stdin"

content.text:
The replacement is complete and correct. Here is a summary of the changes made to `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbw/rbgp_Payor.sh`:

**Changes made (lines 577-608):**

1. **URL-not-set error (lines 579-584)**: Replaced the terse `test -n ... || buc_die` one-liner with a multi-line block that uses `buc_warn` for the problem, `buc_info` for guidance, `buc_next "rbw-Pgl"` to direct users to the setup guide tabtarget, and then `buc_die` with a clear message.

2. **Non-GitLab URL error (lines 589-595)**: Replaced the single long `buc_die` line (which included an inline explanation about GitHub PAT scoping) with a multi-line block using `buc_warn`, `buc_info`, `buc_next "rbw-Pgl"`, and `buc_die` -- moving the rationale out of the error message and into the guide.

3. **Token prompt (lines 598-608)**: Removed the 17-line verbose inline guide (the "Why GitLab?" explanation and "Setup" instructions) and replaced it with a single `buc_info` line showing the rubric repo URL, a terse prompt referencing the guide (`./tt/rbw-Pgl.*`), and a conditional empty-token error that also directs users to the guide via `buc_next`.

The net effect reduces approximately 37 lines to 32 lines, replacing verbose inline documentation with terse error messages that point users to the `rbw-Pgl` tabtarget for detailed setup instructions.
